commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Sedge-bush, or flag. Hebrew achu; so called, because from one root many brothers (as it were) spring. Septuagint style it Boutomon, as it was usually "cut for oxen "Genesis xli. 2. (Parkhurst) (Haydock)
As plants die without suction, so do those who depart from God. (Menochius)

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: To whom Bildad compares ‘the rush’ and ‘the flag,’
